The issue of the 10 to 20% surrender of project costs btw sheds light on an earlier question of mine. Why were certain individuals seeking crowd sourced investment years ago. Seems like a downpayment. Ensure the kickback is paid first then you’ll get the project. During the pandemic, someone in one of my FB chat groups promoted an investment opportunity linked to a construction company, promising returns that would double the initial capital. This individual was connected to a newly established construction firm, despite lacking engineering qualifications or any construction background. At the time, it appeared either to be a scam or a fraudulent scheme potentially enabled through government connections. I discouraged one of my cousins to part from his hard earned cash to participate in it. I told him if it happens to be linked to a corruption which now I think it is, he will not be proud and be happy where the return come from. 

I think many of these newly established construction companies also needs to be investigated, although potentially, these have been linked to the top baggers.

I was surprised when I watched it on the news yesterday, imagine tissue paper pa lang yan, what about other office supplies? In our corporate setting we once had an oversupply of photocopier ribbon cartridges, nag anticipate ng growth in printing requirements due to new systems being introduced. However, sumobra ang dami ng bili to the point natuyo na yun ibang ribbons, say nag expire, sayang. Although this is due to miscalculation, nothing intentional, but for the case of SSS, the tissue issue is quite overwhelming, again tissue pa lang ang issue nyan. Minsan naiisip ko yung 3-year annual increase was a great delight to us pensioners, then this brouhaha happened, di kaya parang naging smokescreen ito at SSS was wary magkaka bukingan, so the miraculous increases in pensions happened? Good na u-ukilkil na mga ganitong mga unscrupulous practises, again sa SSS pa lang yan, I heard sa DAR madami din, lalo na yung mga pinangakong mga makinarya, e.g dryers, para sa mga magsasaka, saan na napunta at tuloy ang pagtutuyo ng mga palay sa kalye, nakaka lungkot mga kababayan ko

The deadline for this problem was decades ago. Our government has always been reactive in addressing problems but proactive in stealing from our Treasury.

During the early 70’s, we have experienced the first global oil shock. Ever since , we have learned that OPEC will weaponize their oil products.

But our leaders have never learned to address the oil problem properly.

The oil deregulation program started on the wrong foot. The new entrants were never given the chance to have a head start to even the playing field. 

Even the weekly oil adjustment that was based on MOPS could not be scrutinized since it is confidential. Thus, there was no transparency.

If my memory serves me right, only Petron has a refinery while everyone imports their oil in finished products.

It is high time to review the oil deregulation law to address its flaws for the benefit of the future generation.

Fuel prices go up, electricity go up, prices of foods and other goods go up. All the while giving Russia more opportunity to fund its war against Ukraine. And as we have millions of Filipimos in the middle east, who knows up to what damage that could be in the inflow of remittances and of course life/safety of our fellow citizens. Even bringing them all back will be a nightmare, how can we with the sheer volume of them eh kahit developed countries are struggling to evacuate theirs. Integrated bar of the Philippines

This is an important conversation. Professional accountability should never depend on public pressure alone.

Kung may malinaw na standards ang isang propesyon, dapat may malinaw ding paninindigan kapag nalalabag ang mga iyon. Respect and dignity—lalo na sa public discourse—should be the baseline, not the exception.

Many lawyers, including women in the profession, work every day to uphold those standards. Their work deserves an institution that speaks just as clearly when the moment calls for it.

Transparency and ethical leadership matter. Silence, especially in moments like this, inevitably raises hard questions.

#Accountability #RuleOfLaw #LegalEthics #RespectAndDignity #WomenInLaw #ProfessionalStandards #Philippines #PublicDiscourse

This is a really eye-opening breakdown. It’s surprising how much of our national security framework is still anchored in laws written generations ago.

Totoo—ibang mundo na ang espionage ngayon. Cyber operations, digital leaks, social engineering… hindi na ito katulad noong 1940s. Kung ganito na ka-advanced ang methods, kailangan talagang sumabay ang batas.

At the same time, stories like this also show how recruitment often targets financially vulnerable people. That doesn’t excuse the act, but it does show why stronger safeguards, better oversight, and clearer deterrence are important.

Hopefully lawmakers move faster on updating these laws. National security frameworks should evolve as fast as the threats they’re trying to address.

#NationalSecurity #EspionageLaw #PhilippineLaw #WestPhilippineSea #PublicPolicy #RuleOfLaw #Philippines #SecurityMatters
